details: The "Rashtriya Khadya Suraksha Yojana" started on 02-10-2013 by the Food and Civil Supplies Department of Rajasthan. This scheme aim is to provide adequate quality food grains at reasonable prices to the people so that they can lead a dignified life. The Act provides for coverage of 75% of the rural population and 50% of the urban population to receive subsidized food grains under the Targeted Public Distribution System (TPDS) which covers about two-thirds of the population.
benefits: Antyodaya families are provided with 35 kg of wheat per family.
Other eligible beneficiaries receive 5 kg of wheat per unit per month.
Ration card holders categorized under Antyodaya and Below Poverty Line (BPL) in the state are entitled to wheat at a subsidized rate of ₹1 per kg.
Other eligible families categorized as Priority Household receive food security at ₹2 per kg per month.
eligibility: The applicant should be a native of Rajasthan.
The applicant should be of Antyodaya/ BPL families/Annapurna.
The applicant should have a ration card.

offline: The applicant need to Downloads the
and get the application form from page no. 4.
Carefully read any instructions or guidelines provided.
Collect all the information and documents.
Make a copy of the completed form for your records before submitting it. This will serve as a reference in case you need to refer back to the information provided.
Submit the form to the concerned Appellate Officer (Sub Division/District Logistics Officer) .

The Appellate Officer will thoroughly scrutinize the application. If complete and correct, the applicant's name will be added to the food security list. However, if doubts arise regarding the presented documents, the officer will only proceed after clarification.
The Appellate Officer will issue an appeal order with a computer-generated digitized signature, automatically updating the applicant's name in the online food security list.

The responsibility of the Appellate Officers is limited to verifying the completeness of the application and attached certificates. If any applicant is found to have provided false information to gain inclusion in the food security list, their name will be canceled, and legal action will be taken.

faqs.question: What is the objective of the Rashtriya Khadya Suraksha Yojana?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to provide adequate quality food grains at affordable rates to the economically weaker sections of Rajasthan to ensure a dignified standard of living. adequate quality food grains economically weaker sections of Rajasthan

faqs.question: Who are the primary beneficiaries of this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme covers: Antyodaya families Below Poverty Line (BPL) families Priority Household members under the Targeted Public Distribution System (TPDS) Antyodaya families Antyodaya families Below Poverty Line (BPL) families Below Poverty Line (BPL) Priority Household members under the Targeted Public Distribution System (TPDS) Priority Household members

faqs.question: What food grain quantity and price are offered under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Antyodaya families: 35 kg wheat per family at ₹1/kg BPL and Priority households: 5 kg wheat per unit per month at ₹2/kg Antyodaya families: 35 kg wheat per family at ₹1/kg Antyodaya families: BPL and Priority households: 5 kg wheat per unit per month at ₹2/kg BPL and Priority households:

faqs.question: What is the coverage of this scheme in Rajasthan?
faqs.answer: It covers 75% of the rural population and 50% of the urban population , targeting nearly two-thirds of the state’s total residents. 75% of the rural population 50% of the urban population

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Applicants must: Be natives of Rajasthan Belong to Antyodaya, BPL, or Priority Household categories Possess a valid ration card Be natives of Rajasthan natives of Rajasthan Belong to Antyodaya, BPL, or Priority Household categories Antyodaya, BPL, or Priority Household Possess a valid ration card ration card

faqs.question: What documents are required to apply?
faqs.answer: Ration Card Aadhaar Card Bhamashah Card Caste Certificate BPL Card (if applicable) Affidavit (available on page 6 of the application form) Ration Card Aadhaar Card Bhamashah Card Caste Certificate BPL Card (if applicable) Affidavit (available on page 6 of the application form)

faqs.question: How can one apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: The application process is offline . Applicants must: Download and fill the form Attach required documents Submit to the Sub Division/District Logistics Officer Await verification and inclusion in the food security list offline Download and fill the form Attach required documents Submit to the Sub Division/District Logistics Officer Sub Division/District Logistics Officer Await verification and inclusion in the food security list

faqs.question: What is the role of the Appellate Officer?
faqs.answer: The Appellate Officer: Verifies submitted forms Approves or rejects based on document authenticity Updates the food security list online using a digitized signature The Appellate Officer: Verifies submitted forms Approves or rejects based on document authenticity Updates the food security list online using a digitized signature digitized signature

faqs.question: Where can the application form be obtained?
faqs.answer: Applicants can download the form from the official document ( refer to page no. 4 ) or collect it from the concerned local office. download the form refer to page no. 4

faqs.question: What happens in case of false information in the application?
faqs.answer: If any applicant is found submitting false or misleading information , their name will be removed from the list , and legal action will be taken. false or misleading information removed from the list legal action
